title: A crypto news desk that would rather say nothing than shill you
dek: Crypto Cronkite launches with one promise: report the real news, strip the paid promotion, and never tell you what to do with your money.
author: The Editor
body: If you follow crypto, you already know the problem. Most of what arrives dressed as news is really an ad. A token you have never heard of is going to 100x by Friday. A vague partnership is announced by the only party who benefits from announcing it. A list of coins to buy turns out to be an affiliate link with a headline. It is relentless, and it is how ordinary people end up holding the bag.

Crypto Cronkite exists to be the opposite of that. The scarce thing in this space is not another price feed or another hot take. It is an honest voice. So that is the whole product: report what actually happened, strip out the shill, and never once tell you to buy or sell anything.

Walter Cronkite was trusted for a simple reason. He was straight with people. He told them what he knew, told them how he knew it, and did not pretend to know more than he did. That is the register we are holding ourselves to. When we are sure, we will say so plainly. When we are not, we will say that too.

body.h2: The machine does the grind, a human owns the judgment

body: Here is how the desk actually works, because you should not have to take our word for it. Every day, software pulls the real stories from many sources at once and weights official and primary sources most. An AI editor ranks them by genuine significance and strips the obvious promotion. Then a second, independent AI checks each story against its cited sources and tries to prove it wrong. Only after all of that does a human read the queue, override the machine where judgment differs, add the honest take, and approve what publishes.

That last step is not decoration. It is the point. The machine is the newsroom staff. The human is the editor-in-chief. Nothing goes out as reporting, and nothing goes out as a take, without a person signing their name to it. If keeping that up ever stops being sustainable, we will slow the cadence before we lower the bar. A daily that dies is worth less than a twice-a-week you can trust.

body.h2: What you can hold us to

body: We will link every source. We will mark what we could not verify. We will correct our mistakes in the open. And we will never run paid coverage as news, because paid coverage as news is the exact thing we were built to strip out.

We are not your financial advisor, and nothing here is investment advice. We will tell you what happened and, carefully, what it may mean. What you do with that is yours. That is the deal. Welcome to Crypto Cronkite.
human_take: The honest edge in crypto is not being first. It is being trustworthy. If we ever have to choose between the two, we choose trustworthy, every time.
